You are hereby notified that the unauthorized disclosure or other unlawful use of this fax or any personal health information is prohibited. To the extent patient information contained in this fax is subject to 42 CFR Part 2, this regulation prohibits unauthorized disclosure of these records. If you received this fax in error, please visit www.athenahealth.com/NotMyFax to notify the sender and confirm that the information will be destroyed. If you do not have internet access, please call 1-888-482-8436 to notify the sender and confirm that the information will be destroyed. Thank you for your attention and cooperation. Other?: Biopsy (Notes: was done before mammograms and was benign). 03-09-2026 1:25 PM ET athena 14809560654 pg 9 of 12 FAMILY PRACTICE SPECIALISTS 4600 E Shea blvd, PHOENIX AZ 85028-6031 DAVIS, Barbara H (id #50059, dob: 08/03/1936) Do you have any first degree relatives with breat cancer?: N. Have you ever had a bone mineral density test?: Y (Notes: Osteoporosis). Most Recent Bone Density: 02/17/2017. Have you ever broken any bones: Y (Notes: 1999, fell of a hore). Do you take: Osteoporosis Medication (Notes: Prolia). How many years?: 5. Do you ever have back pain?: Y. Symptoms are: Mild/Severe. Date of Last Mammogram: 01/02/2018. Obstetric History Obstetric History not reviewed (last reviewed 12/12/2025) Past Medical History Past Medical History not reviewed (last reviewed 12/12/2025) Last Bone Density: Y - 02/17/2017 Last Colonoscopy: Y - 04/25/2011, 04/29/2016 negative Last Complete Physical: Y - 01/10/2019, WWE 11/28/18; 1/14/20, 9/14/2023, 10/21/2024 Last EGD: Y 09/10/2010, 04/29/2016 Last Eye Exam (Retinal): Y - 05/18/2017, 6/29/2023, 9/18/2024 negative Last Mammogram: Y - 08/2016, 01/02/18 Last Pap Smear: Y - 10/2012, 05/01/2015 OB/GYN, 10/2016 OB/GYN Notes: Cardiac Ablation 7/2015 Documents for Discussion Discussed the following documents: NP Thyroid 30 mg tablet 01/19/26 Screening None recorded. HPI Pt is a 89 year old female with PMH of HTN, hyperlipidemia, IBS, OAB, and hypothyroidism who presents for follow up. Medications were reviewed and reconciled. Taking as prescribed with no adverse reactions. Feeling well. She c/o fatigue, but is otherwise, doing well. No other complaints. ROS Patient reports fatigue but reports no fever, no chills, no night sweats, and no significant weight gain/loss. She reports constipation but reports no abdominal pain, no nausea, no vomiting, no heartburn, no bloating, and no difficulty swallowing. She reports increased frequency, leaking urine, and incontinence but reports no pain during urination, no urgency, no blood in urine, and no flank pain. She reports dizziness but reports no vertigo, no loss of consciousness, no memory loss, no numbness, no tingling, no headaches, and no seizures; aphasia. She reports anxiety but reports no depression and no sleep disturbance. She reports no eye irritation and no vision change. She reports no chest pain and no rapid or irregular heartbeat. She reports no shortness of breath, no wheezing, and no cough. She reports no vaginal lesions, no vaginal discharge, no vaginal itching or burning, and no vaginal odor. She reports menopause. She reports no muscle aches, no muscle cramps, no muscle weakness, no joint pains, and no back pain. She reports no rash, no skin ulcers, no abnormal moles, and no nail discoloration. She reports no breast lumps, no breast pain, and no nipple discharge. She reports no heat/cold intolerance, no increased thirst, no abnormal hair loss/growth, and no change in libido. She reports no swollen glands, no bruising, and no bleeding disorder. Physical Exam Constitutional: General Appearance: well-nourished and well-developed. Level of Distress: NAD. Ambulation: ambulating normally. Head: Head: normocephalic and atraumatic. Eyes: Lids and Conjunctivae: no discharge or pallor and non-injected. Pupils: PERRLA. EOM: EOMI. Sclerae: non- icteric. Vision: peripheral vision grossly intact and acuity grossly intact. Ear, Nose, Mouth, and Throat: Ears: no lesions on external ear and normal hearing. Nose: no lesions on external nose or nasal discharge. Mouth: no lip lesions/ulcers. Neck: Neck: supple, trachea midline, and no masses. Thyroid: no enlargement. Repiratory: Effort: no dyspnea. Auscultation: clear to auscultation. 03-09-2026 1:25 PM ET athena 14809560654 pg 10 of 12 FAMILY PRACTICE SPECIALISTS 4600 E Shea blvd, PHOENIX AZ 85028-6031 DAVIS, Barbara H (id #50059, dob: 08/03/1936) Cardiovascular: Neck Vessels: no carotid bruits. Pulses: normal throughout. Extremities: no cyanosis, edema, varicosities, or palpable cord. Gastrointestinal: Bowel Sounds: normal bowel sounds. Inspection and Palpation: no masses, tenderness (no guarding, no rebound), or CVA tenderness and soft and non-distended. Liver: non-tender and no hepatomegaly. Spleen: non-tender and no splenomegaly. Hernia: none palpable. Musculoskeletal:: Musculoskeletal: no contractures, tenderness, or bony abnormalities and normal range of motion. Back: Back: normal curvature. Neurologic: Gait and Station: shuffling. Cranial Nerves: grossly intact. Motor Strength and Tone: normal strength and tone. Sensation: grossly intact. Reflexes: normal DTRs throughout. Coordination and Cerebellum: no tremor. Skin: Inspection and palpation: no rash, lacerations, scars, lesions, ulcers, induration, nodules, or jaundice and good turgor. Nails: normal. Lymphatic: Cervical: no enlargement or tenderness. Supraclavicular: no enlargement or tenderness. Inguinal: no enlargement or tenderness. Psychiatric: Insight: good judgement. Mental Status: normal mood and affect and active and alert. Orientation: to time, place, and person. Memory: recent memory normal and remote memory normal. Assessment / Plan Agrees to medical student. 1. Aphasia - Worsening. Recent blood work within normal limits. Neurological exam negative. Updated MRI 3/4/2025 demonstrates chronic small vessel ischemic changes. S/P SLP. Followed by neurology. DAT Scan negative. R47.01: Aphasia 2. CVD (cerebrovascular disease) As above. However, the hyperintense T2 change in periventricular and subcortical bilateral white matter changes noted on MRI may also be a result of migraine headaches. Neurology to address. ASA 81 mg daily. 167.9: Cerebrovascular disease, unspecified 3. PSP (progressive supranuclear palsy) P-tau217 positive. However, neurology is more concerned for PSP as the diagnosis. On WAVi, her physical reaction time is slow. Peak frequency is slow in all areas. Her P300 is slow as well. Findings most c/w a neurodegenerative condition, such as Parkinson's disease or PSP. G23.1: Progressive supranuclear ophthalmoplegia [Steele-Richardson-Olszewski] 4. Autonomic dysfunction Stable. Suspect autonomic dysfunction. CARTs confirms. Followed by cardiology.
